steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The text argues that no single factor fully explains pedestrian habits in Vancouver — demographics, commute distance, and amenities all play a role. The correct choice must reflect that pedestrian amenities are just one of several contributing factors, not the dominant one.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — amenities reduce walkability elsewhere: the text never claims amenities harm other cities' walkability; this is unsupported. ✗
  • Choice B — amenities cause demographics: reverses the causal direction the text implies. ✗
  • Choice C — demographics rank above amenities: the text explicitly says no single factor dominates, so ranking any factor first contradicts the passage. ✗
  • Choice D — amenities are one of several factors: matches the text's core point that multiple factors each contribute and no one element transfers walkability alone. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
